question: Zoro vowed to become the world's greatest swordsman for the sake of a childhood rival who died young at his dojo. Who was she?
explainer: Kuina was the dojo master's daughter who always beat Zoro; after she died in an accident, Zoro carried on their shared dream of being the greatest swordsman.

question: In Loguetown, Zoro tested whether a cursed blade would accept him by tossing it spinning over his outstretched arm. Which sword was it?
explainer: The cursed Sandai Kitetsu spun down but missed Zoro's arm, so he accepted it, betting his luck against its curse.

question: With only enough money to pay Arlong's tribute for one person, how did Bell-mère choose to spend it before Arlong killed her?
correct_answer: She publicly claimed Nami and Nojiko as her own daughters
incorrect_answers: She paid the tribute for the two girls instead of herself
She opened fire on Arlong with her rifle
She hid the girls in the tangerine grove
options: She paid the tribute for the two girls instead of herself
She publicly claimed Nami and Nojiko as her own daughters
She opened fire on Arlong with her rifle
She hid the girls in the tangerine grove
explainer: Bell-mère could have denied the girls to save herself, but she refused to disown them as her family and was shot for it.

question: Usopp's father, Yasopp, left Syrup Village behind to sail as a marksman for which crew?
explainer: Yasopp is Shanks' sharpshooter; Shanks even tells Luffy about him, connecting the two families.

question: Which pirate captain spent three years posing as the butler 'Klahadore' to scheme his way into Kaya's fortune?
explainer: Captain Kuro of the Black Cat Pirates faked his own death and infiltrated Kaya's household as the butler Klahadore to inherit her wealth.

question: Stranded on a barren rock, Zeff secretly gave young Sanji all of their food. How did Zeff himself survive the wait for rescue?
incorrect_answers: He fished from the surrounding rocks
He rationed a hidden chest of food
options: He fished from the surrounding rocks
He rationed a hidden chest of food
explainer: Sanji discovered Zeff's bag held treasure, not food, realizing the cook had eaten his own leg so the boy could have every scrap.

question: At the Baratie, which Warlord effortlessly defeated Zoro, becoming the rival Zoro must one day surpass?
explainer: Mihawk, the world's greatest swordsman, sought out Zoro and beat him decisively, telling him to grow stronger and seek him again.

question: Buggy and Shanks both got their start as apprentices aboard which legendary crew?
explainer: Both served as cabin-boy-era crewmates under Gol D. Roger, which is why Buggy's grudge against Shanks runs so deep.

question: Buggy the Clown can split his body into floating pieces thanks to which Devil Fruit?
explainer: The Bara Bara no Mi lets Buggy separate his body at will, making him immune to slashing attacks like Zoro's swords.

question: After eating the Sube Sube no Mi, the pirate Alvida gained slippery skin along with what dramatic change to her appearance?
explainer: A side effect of the Smooth-Smooth Fruit shed Alvida's fat along with everything else that slid off her, leaving her slim and smooth-skinned.

question: Zoro was strung up for execution at the Shells Town Marine base ruled by which tyrannical officer?
explainer: Captain 'Axe-Hand' Morgan terrorized Shells Town; Luffy freed Zoro and helped topple him in the arc that formed the crew's first partnership.

question: In Loguetown, Buggy nearly executed Luffy on the very platform where Gol D. Roger died. What suddenly saved Luffy's life?
correct_answer: A bolt of lightning struck the execution platform
incorrect_answers: Zoro cut Buggy down from behind
options: A bolt of lightning struck the execution platform
Zoro cut Buggy down from behind
explainer: A sudden storm sent lightning into the scaffold as the blade fell, destroying it and sparing a grinning Luffy.

question: The Marine captain Smoker, who first hunts Luffy in Loguetown, turns his body into smoke using which Logia fruit?
explainer: Smoker's Moku Moku no Mi lets him become and control smoke, which he uses to pin Luffy in Loguetown.

question: A mysterious man shields Luffy from Smoker's grasp as he escapes Loguetown. He is later revealed to lead which organization?
incorrect_answers: The Seven Warlords of the Sea
options: The Seven Warlords of the Sea
explainer: The stranger is Monkey D. Dragon, Luffy's father and leader of the Revolutionary Army that opposes the World Government.

question: Luffy earned the first bounty of his career after defeating which East Blue pirate?
explainer: Freeing Cocoyasi Village from the Arlong Pirates earned Luffy his first bounty, an unusually high one for a rookie.

question: Princess Vivi infiltrated Baroque Works to expose Crocodile using which agent codename?
explainer: Vivi posed as the low-ranking agent Miss Wednesday, partnered with Igaram, to uncover Baroque Works' plot against Alabasta.

question: Crocodile can dissolve into sand and drain the moisture from his enemies thanks to which Devil Fruit?
explainer: The Logia Suna Suna no Mi turns Crocodile into sand and lets him dehydrate anything he touches, a deadly edge in the desert of Alabasta.

question: Before she ever joined the Straw Hats, Nico Robin served Crocodile as vice-president of Baroque Works under which codename?
explainer: Robin operated as Miss All Sunday, Crocodile's right hand, first appearing in Alabasta before later joining Luffy's crew.

question: Ace is introduced during the Alabasta saga while tracking a target. Which crew does he serve as a division commander?
explainer: Ace is the 2nd division commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, and reunites with his brother Luffy in Alabasta.

question: In Alabasta, Ace tells Luffy he is hunting a traitor who murdered one of Whitebeard's commanders. Who is Ace pursuing?
explainer: Ace is chasing Marshall D. Teach, better known as Blackbeard, a former Whitebeard subordinate who killed a fellow commander and fled.

question: Who used a falcon Zoan power to fly the giant bomb high above Alubarna, sacrificing himself to save the city?
explainer: Pell, one of Alabasta's royal guardians, transformed via the Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon and carried the bomb skyward as it detonated.

question: Crocodile's real aim in seizing Alabasta was to uncover the location of which ancient weapon rumored to be hidden there?
explainer: Behind his civil-war scheme, Crocodile sought the ancient weapon Pluton, whose whereabouts are recorded on a hidden Poneglyph in Alabasta.

question: In Mock Town, Luffy and Zoro take a beating without fighting back until which pirate captain mocks the very idea of chasing dreams?
explainer: Bellamy 'the Hyena' ridicules those who believe in the City of Gold; Luffy endures his beating and later fells him with a single punch.

question: Montblanc Cricket, who dredges the sea for a lost city of gold, is a descendant of which man executed as a liar four centuries earlier?
explainer: Noland told his king of a city of gold that had vanished, and was beheaded as a liar; Cricket is his outcast descendant trying to clear the family name.

question: The golden city of Shandora did not sink as everyone believed. What actually happened to that part of the island?
correct_answer: It was launched into the sky by the Knock-Up Stream
incorrect_answers: It sank beneath the Grand Line
It was buried under volcanic ash
It was seized by the World Government
options: It was launched into the sky by the Knock-Up Stream
It sank beneath the Grand Line
It was buried under volcanic ash
It was seized by the World Government
explainer: A chunk of Jaya carrying the golden city was blasted up to the White Sea by the Knock-Up Stream, becoming Upper Yard in Skypiea.

question: Enel, who proclaims himself a god over Skypiea, wields which Logia Devil Fruit?
explainer: The Goro Goro no Mi lets Enel become and control lightning, the source of his god-like power over Skypiea.

question: Enel intended to abandon Skypiea and travel to a boundless promised land he called what?
explainer: Enel dreamed of reaching Fairy Vearth, the 'endless land'; a cover-story arc reveals it to be the moon.

question: The warrior Wyper fights to reclaim Upper Yard on behalf of which people, the descendants of Shandora?
explainer: The Shandia are the descendants of Shandora's people and have fought for 400 years to reclaim their ancestral homeland, now Upper Yard.

question: Enel's four priests could predict attacks using an ability later revealed to be an early form of Observation Haki. What was it called?
explainer: In Skypiea it is called Mantra; the story later confirms this is the same power as Kenbunshoku (Observation) Haki.

question: At Skypiea's climax, how did Luffy prove that Noland had told the truth about the golden city?
correct_answer: He rang the island's long-silent Golden Bell
incorrect_answers: He recovered a solid gold pillar
He read the ancient Poneglyph aloud
He defeated the false god Gan Fall
options: He rang the island's long-silent Golden Bell
He recovered a solid gold pillar
He read the ancient Poneglyph aloud
He defeated the false god Gan Fall
explainer: Luffy strikes the Golden Bell of Shandora so its toll rings out, the very 'song of the island' Noland once heard, finally reaching Cricket below.

question: Beside the Poneglyph at the golden belfry, Robin discovers a carved message proving that which figure had reached Skypiea?
explainer: Robin finds that Gol D. Roger left his own inscription on the Poneglyph, revealing the Pirate King had once stood in Skypiea.

question: Water 7 braces every year for a catastrophic tidal surge known by what name?
explainer: Aqua Laguna is the annual high tide that floods the low-lying city of water, forcing residents to evacuate to higher ground.

question: Nico Robin is the sole survivor of the destruction of which island, home to the world's greatest scholars?
explainer: Robin's home island of Ohara was wiped out by a Buster Call for researching the forbidden Void Century; she alone escaped.

question: Rob Lucci, Kaku, and Blueno secretly served which covert World Government agency at Enies Lobby?
explainer: CP9 is an illegal assassination branch of Cipher Pol, licensed to kill in the name of Justice, embedded undercover in Water 7.

question: Which Devil Fruit gives Rob Lucci his powerful leopard-hybrid form?
correct_answer: Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
incorrect_answers: Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Giraffe
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Wolf
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
options: Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Giraffe
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Wolf
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
explainer: Lucci ate the Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard, a Carnivorous Zoan that makes him a ferocious leopard-man.

question: When the swordsman Kaku was made to eat a Zoan fruit at Enies Lobby, he transformed into which unexpected animal?
explainer: Kaku ate the Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Giraffe, leaving him dismayed at his awkward new giraffe-man body.

question: CP9 agents rate a fighter's raw combat strength using which unit of measurement?
explainer: Doriki is CP9's numerical measure of martial power; the elite Rob Lucci registers an unheard-of 4000 Doriki.

question: Franky and Iceburg both trained as apprentices under which master shipwright, the builder of the Pirate King's ship?
explainer: The legendary fish-man shipwright Tom mentored both Iceburg and Cutty Flam (Franky) in his Water 7 workshop.

question: The shipwright Tom was condemned by the World Government for the 'crime' of building whose ship?
explainer: Tom built the Oro Jackson, the ship that carried Gol D. Roger; constructing the Pirate King's vessel was treated as a capital crime.

question: After the Straw Hats declared war on the World Government at Enies Lobby, Luffy's bounty leapt to what amount?
explainer: Luffy's bounty tripled from 100 million to 300 million Berries after his crew defied a Buster Call at Enies Lobby.

question: The World Government first placed a bounty on Nico Robin's head at what shockingly young age?
explainer: Robin was branded a criminal with a bounty at just 8 years old after surviving Ohara, feared for her ability to read Poneglyphs.

question: Which giant former Marine defied the World Government to help a young Robin escape the Buster Call on Ohara?
explainer: The giant Vice Admiral Saul turned against his orders to save Robin and taught her to laugh; he was frozen by his friend Kuzan (Aokiji).

question: Usopp temporarily left the Straw Hats after a bitter duel with Luffy over what?
correct_answer: Whether to keep the battered Going Merry
incorrect_answers: Who should be the crew's captain
How to split the Skypiea gold
Whether to surrender Robin to CP9
options: Whether to keep the battered Going Merry
Who should be the crew's captain
How to split the Skypiea gold
Whether to surrender Robin to CP9
explainer: When the crew decided the damaged Going Merry could no longer sail, Usopp refused to let it go and challenged Luffy to a duel before leaving.

question: Spandam's sword Funkfreed was extraordinary because it could transform into what animal?
explainer: Funkfreed was fed the Zou Zou no Mi, letting the sword itself become an elephant, one of the first weapons shown to eat a Devil Fruit.

question: Long before he joined the Straw Hats, Brook served as musician aboard a crew that made a promise to a whale at Reverse Mountain. Who captained that crew?
explainer: The Rumbar Pirates were led by Calico Yorki; Brook served as the crew's musician before disease and misfortune wiped them out.

question: When Brook's soul finally found its way back to his skeleton, he was enormously relieved that his afro had survived. Why did that hairstyle matter so much to him?
correct_answer: So that Laboon would still recognize him
incorrect_answers: So that his crew could identify his corpse
Because his hair shielded his skull from harm
So he could keep a blade hidden inside it
options: So that his crew could identify his corpse
So that Laboon would still recognize him
Because his hair shielded his skull from harm
So he could keep a blade hidden inside it
explainer: Brook clung to the hope of reuniting with the whale Laboon and was glad his distinctive afro stayed intact so Laboon would know him.

question: Gecko Moria could tear the shadows off people and sew them into corpses to build a zombie army. What Devil Fruit gave him that power?
explainer: The Kage Kage no Mi lets Moria manipulate shadows, ripping them from victims and implanting them into the dead.

question: Moria's ultimate weapon on Thriller Bark was the reanimated corpse of a legendary giant said to have once dragged whole islands around. Who was this ancient giant?
explainer: Oars was an ancient demon-giant of legend; Moria revived his corpse and implanted Luffy's shadow to command him.

question: Bartholomew Kuma can repel anything he touches with the paw pads on his hands, even launching people clear across the world. Name his Devil Fruit.
explainer: The Nikyu Nikyu no Mi gives Kuma paw pads that repel matter, air, pain, and even fatigue at high speed.

question: Perona, Moria's subordinate on Thriller Bark, tormented intruders with ghosts that could drain a person's will to live. What is her Devil Fruit?
explainer: Perona's Horo Horo no Mi produces ghosts, including 'Negative Hollows' that sap victims' morale.

question: Years before Thriller Bark, Gecko Moria lost his entire crew in a single catastrophic New World battle, an event that broke him and drove him to build zombies. Which Emperor crushed that crew?
explainer: Moria's crew was annihilated by Kaido in the New World; the trauma pushed him toward an army of expendable, shadow-powered zombies.

question: Brook endured decades alone as a living skeleton to honor a promise to return to a companion waiting at Reverse Mountain. Who was waiting?
explainer: The Rumbar Pirates promised to come back to the whale Laboon at the Twin Capes; Brook, the last survivor, carried that promise for fifty years.

question: After the Straw Hats collapsed exhausted from beating Moria, Bartholomew Kuma agreed to spare them if he could take Luffy's life. Which crewmate offered his own life in Luffy's place?
explainer: Zoro told Kuma to take his head instead of Luffy's; Kuma instead transferred Luffy's accumulated pain and fatigue into him.

question: The powerful zombie general Brook dueled on Thriller Bark was animated by Brook's own stolen shadow and inhabited the corpse of a legendary samurai. Name that samurai.
explainer: Moria placed Brook's shadow into the corpse of Ryuma, a legendary Wano swordsman, creating the zombie Zombie Ryuma.

question: At the Sabaody auction house, Luffy threw a punch that horrified the whole archipelago and drew an admiral down on the Supernovas. Which Celestial Dragon did he hit?
explainer: Luffy punched Saint Charloss for shooting Hatchan, an unthinkable offense against a World Noble that summoned Admiral Kizaru and the Pacifistas.

question: When the eleven Supernovas converged on Sabaody Archipelago, one of them carried the highest bounty of the group. Who was it?
explainer: At Sabaody, Eustass Kid held the largest bounty among the Supernovas, edging out Luffy for the top spot.

question: When Kuma scattered the Straw Hats across the world at Sabaody, Luffy was flung onto an island where men are forbidden. Where did he land?
explainer: Kuma sent Luffy to Amazon Lily, home of the all-female Kuja tribe, while Nami went to Weatheria, Usopp to the Boin Archipelago, and Zoro to Kuraigana Island.

question: Boa Hancock can turn anyone who feels desire toward her into stone. What Devil Fruit gives her this ability?
explainer: The Mero Mero no Mi petrifies those who harbor lust or attraction, weaponizing Hancock's beauty.

question: The Boa sisters hide a shameful mark on their backs, telling their people it is the curse of the Gorgon. What is that mark really?
incorrect_answers: The emblem of the Sun Pirates
The royal crest of the Kuja tribe
options: The emblem of the Sun Pirates
The royal crest of the Kuja tribe
explainer: Hancock and her sisters were enslaved by Celestial Dragons and bear the Hoof of the Soaring Dragon brand, which they concealed by claiming it was a Gorgon's curse.

question: Boa Hancock rules Amazon Lily as its empress. What royal title do her people, the Kuja, use for her?
explainer: Hancock is known to the Kuja as the Snake Princess (Hebihime), ruler of Amazon Lily and captain of the Kuja Pirates.

question: Magellan, the dreaded warden of Impel Down, nearly killed Luffy with a torrent of lethal poison. What Devil Fruit does he wield?
explainer: Magellan's Doku Doku no Mi lets him generate and control deadly poisons, which he used to devastate Luffy in Impel Down.

question: During the Impel Down breakout, the okama Bentham stayed behind to hold a crucial gate open so the escapees could reach the sea, sacrificing himself. By what codename was this Baroque Works veteran better known?
explainer: Bentham, the former Baroque Works agent Mr. 2 Bon Clay, stayed behind to keep the gate open for the escaping ship, apparently sacrificing himself.

question: Whitebeard could crack the very air and shatter the ground with quakes, earning him the reputation of the strongest man in the world. What Devil Fruit did he have?
explainer: Whitebeard's Gura Gura no Mi let him generate earthquakes and quake-shockwaves powerful enough to destroy the world.

question: To justify Ace's public execution at Marineford, Sengoku revealed the identity of Ace's true father to the world. Who was it?
explainer: Sengoku announced that Ace was the son of the late Pirate King Gol D. Roger, framing his execution as erasing Roger's bloodline.

question: At the climax of Marineford, Ace died taking a fatal blow meant for Luffy. Which Marine admiral struck him down?
explainer: Admiral Akainu goaded Ace and then drove a magma fist through him as Ace shielded Luffy, killing him.

question: Moments after Whitebeard fell at Marineford, Blackbeard stunned the world by wielding a second Devil Fruit power. Which power did he claim from the fallen Emperor?
explainer: Blackbeard somehow took Whitebeard's Gura Gura no Mi from his corpse, becoming the only known person to wield two Devil Fruits at once.

question: Why did Fisher Tiger tattoo the mark of the sun over the bodies of the slaves he freed from Mariejois?
correct_answer: To hide the Celestial Dragons' slave brand on freed slaves.
incorrect_answers: To mark new recruits of the Sun Pirates.
To honor Queen Otohime's peace movement.
To intimidate the humans of the Grand Line.
options: To mark new recruits of the Sun Pirates.
To hide the Celestial Dragons' slave brand on freed slaves.
To honor Queen Otohime's peace movement.
To intimidate the humans of the Grand Line.
explainer: Fisher Tiger stamped the sun symbol over each slave's Hoof of the Soaring Dragon brand so no one could tell who had been an escaped slave.

question: After being fatally wounded, how did Fisher Tiger ultimately meet his end?
correct_answer: He refused a human blood transfusion and bled to death.
incorrect_answers: He was executed publicly at Marineford.
options: He was executed publicly at Marineford.
He refused a human blood transfusion and bled to death.
explainer: Consumed by hatred born of his enslavement, Fisher Tiger rejected a blood transfusion offered by humans and died from his wounds.

question: Queen Otohime, who campaigned to move the fish-men to the surface, was secretly assassinated by whom?
explainer: Hody Jones shot Queen Otohime, while a human arsonist was framed as the culprit to inflame anti-human sentiment.

question: What makes Hody Jones's hatred of humans strikingly different from Arlong's?
correct_answer: He never personally suffered under humans; his hatred was pure ideology.
incorrect_answers: Humans murdered his parents in front of him.
He was enslaved by the Celestial Dragons as a child.
A human friend once betrayed him.
options: He never personally suffered under humans; his hatred was pure ideology.
Humans murdered his parents in front of him.
He was enslaved by the Celestial Dragons as a child.
A human friend once betrayed him.
explainer: Hody had no personal grievance against humans; his prejudice was inherited baseless hatred, which he himself admits.

question: Vander Decken IX can hurl any object so it homes in relentlessly on a target he has touched. Which Devil Fruit grants this?
explainer: The Mato Mato no Mi (Target-Target Fruit) lets Decken 'mark' a target by touch so anything he throws will chase them forever, which fueled his obsession with Shirahoshi.

question: After Fisher Tiger's death, Jinbe went on to captain which crew?
explainer: Jinbe became the second captain of the Sun Pirates following Fisher Tiger's death, later accepting a position as a Warlord.

question: The ancient ship Noah is bound to an unfulfilled promise made long ago to the Poseidon of that era. Who made that promise?
explainer: A poneglyph in Ryugu Palace records Joy Boy's apology to the Poseidon of the Void Century for a promise he could not keep, tied to the ship Noah.

question: Princess Shirahoshi is revealed during the arc to secretly be what?
incorrect_answers: A giant Sea King in human form
options: A giant Sea King in human form
explainer: Shirahoshi is the current incarnation of the ancient weapon Poseidon, able to summon and command the Sea Kings.

question: Roughly how deep beneath the sea does Fish-Man Island lie, at the base of the Red Line?
explainer: Fish-Man Island rests about 10,000 meters below sea level, in a zone of eternal darkness lit by the Sunlight Tree Eve.

question: Trafalgar Law's signature 'Room', in which he can freely rearrange and swap anything inside a sphere, comes from which Devil Fruit?
explainer: The Ope Ope no Mi makes Law a 'Surgeon of Death', letting him cut, swap, and manipulate anything within his Room.

question: Who fed Law the Ope Ope no Mi as a child and died to give him a future?
explainer: Rosinante, codenamed Corazon and secretly a Marine, stole the Ope Ope no Mi and fed it to Law to cure him, then was shot dead by his own brother Doflamingo.

question: The Ope Ope no Mi's ultimate 'Perennial Youth Surgery' can grant a person eternal life, but demands what of the user?
correct_answer: It costs the user their own life.
incorrect_answers: The user can never use the fruit again.
The user loses all their memories.
The user is turned permanently to stone.
options: It costs the user their own life.
The user can never use the fruit again.
The user loses all their memories.
The user is turned permanently to stone.
explainer: The surgery grants eternal youth to another person at the cost of the user's own life, which is exactly what Doflamingo wanted from Law.

question: Law's hometown of Flevance was wiped out after its citizens fell fatally ill from what?
explainer: Flevance prospered on toxic Amber Lead ore; the non-contagious Amber Lead Syndrome killed its people, and the government massacred the rest, falsely claiming it was infectious.

question: Beyond turning people into toys, what other cruel effect does Sugar's Hobi Hobi no Mi have?
correct_answer: It erases everyone's memories of the victim.
incorrect_answers: It steals the victim's Devil Fruit power.
It ages the victim into an old person.
It forces the victim to obey Sugar.
options: It erases everyone's memories of the victim.
It steals the victim's Devil Fruit power.
It ages the victim into an old person.
It forces the victim to obey Sugar.
explainer: When Sugar turns someone into a toy, every person who knew them instantly forgets they ever existed; knocking her unconscious reverses all transformations.

question: Rebecca's father, a legendary gladiator turned into a toy soldier by Sugar, is who?
explainer: Kyros, Dressrosa's greatest gladiator and Rebecca's father, was transformed into a one-legged toy soldier, erased from everyone's memory.

question: After his family lost their Celestial Dragon status, what did a young Doflamingo do to try to force his way back into Mariejois?
correct_answer: He killed his own father and presented the head to the World Nobles.
incorrect_answers: He handed his younger brother over as a slave.
He set fire to the island of Mariejois.
options: He killed his own father and presented the head to the World Nobles.
He handed his younger brother over as a slave.
He set fire to the island of Mariejois.
explainer: Doflamingo murdered his father Homing and offered his head to the World Nobles as an apology, hoping to be reinstated, but was rejected.

question: In the underworld, Doflamingo brokered weapons and artificial fruits under which alias?
explainer: Doflamingo secretly operated as the powerful underworld broker 'Joker', supplying weapons and SMILE fruits despite being a public Warlord.

question: The artificial SMILE Devil Fruits produced through Punk Hazard were meant to build whose army?
explainer: SMILEs, made using Caesar's SAD, were sold by Doflamingo to Kaido to create an army of artificial Zoan users.

question: Fighters battled in the Corrida Colosseum to claim which prize Devil Fruit?
explainer: Doflamingo offered the Mera Mera no Mi, Ace's former flame fruit, as the grand prize of the Corrida Colosseum tournament.

question: The victims turned into toys across Dressrosa were all restored the moment Sugar was knocked out. Who took her down?
explainer: Usopp knocked Sugar unconscious with a horrifying face, breaking the Hobi Hobi no Mi's effect and freeing every toy at once, earning him the title 'God Usopp'.

question: Punk Hazard is a bizarre island split into which two extreme halves?
correct_answer: A blazing fire half and a frozen ice half.
incorrect_answers: A scorching desert half and a jungle half.
An ocean half and a floating sky-island half.
A rocky mountain half and a swamp half.
options: A blazing fire half and a frozen ice half.
A scorching desert half and a jungle half.
An ocean half and a floating sky-island half.
A rocky mountain half and a swamp half.
explainer: Punk Hazard was left half-burning and half-frozen after the ten-day duel between Admirals Akainu and Aokiji.

question: After toppling Doflamingo and Dressrosa, Luffy's bounty leapt to what figure?
explainer: Following the Dressrosa arc, Luffy's bounty rose to 500,000,000 Berries as the World Government recognized him as a rising threat.

question: Why was Sanji born without the emotionless, enhanced physiology that his Vinsmoke brothers all share?
correct_answer: His mother Sora took a drug during pregnancy that undid Judge's modifications
incorrect_answers: Judge deliberately kept him unmodified as an experimental control
He was born before Judge perfected the enhancement procedure
Reiju secretly reversed his modifications while he was an infant
options: His mother Sora took a drug during pregnancy that undid Judge's modifications
Judge deliberately kept him unmodified as an experimental control
He was born before Judge perfected the enhancement procedure
Reiju secretly reversed his modifications while he was an infant
explainer: Sora secretly took a drug meant to counter Judge's genetic engineering, which is why Sanji alone retained normal human emotions.

question: Big Mom brings her 'homies' like Zeus and Prometheus to life by using her Devil Fruit to place what inside objects?
explainer: The Soru Soru no Mi lets Big Mom extract soul fragments and place them into objects or animals, animating them as homies.

question: During his duel with Luffy, Charlotte Katakuri revealed his advanced Observation Haki lets him do what?
correct_answer: See a short distance into the future
incorrect_answers: Sense enemies across the entire island
options: See a short distance into the future
Sense enemies across the entire island
explainer: Katakuri's mastery of Observation Haki lets him glimpse moments into the future, which Luffy eventually learns to replicate.

question: What makes Katakuri's Mochi Mochi no Mi unusual among Devil Fruits?
correct_answer: A Paramecia that behaves like a Logia — he can turn his whole body into mochi
incorrect_answers: It lets him absorb and copy other Devil Fruit powers
It grants him a second heart that resists fatal damage
It only works while he is touching the ground
options: A Paramecia that behaves like a Logia — he can turn his whole body into mochi
It lets him absorb and copy other Devil Fruit powers
It grants him a second heart that resists fatal damage
It only works while he is touching the ground
explainer: The Mochi Mochi no Mi is classified as a special Paramecia because Katakuri can turn his whole body into mochi, mimicking a Logia's intangibility.

question: Who formally severed ties with Big Mom's crew during the Whole Cake Island arc to eventually become a Straw Hat?
explainer: Jinbe declared his departure from the Big Mom Pirates to Big Mom's face during the arc, later officially joining the Straw Hats in Wano.

question: What does Capone Bege's Shiro Shiro no Mi allow him to do?
correct_answer: Turn his body into a fortress that houses people and weaponry
incorrect_answers: Harden his skin into impenetrable iron
Shrink himself and others to miniature size
Command an army of ghost soldiers
options: Turn his body into a fortress that houses people and weaponry
Harden his skin into impenetrable iron
Shrink himself and others to miniature size
Command an army of ghost soldiers
explainer: The Shiro Shiro no Mi turns Bege into a living castle, letting him carry troops and cannons inside his body.

question: What secret about herself did Charlotte Pudding desperately hide, having been mocked for it her whole life?
correct_answer: A third eye on her forehead
incorrect_answers: A pair of horns beneath her hair
Fish-man ancestry on her mother's side
A tail she kept tucked away
options: A third eye on her forehead
A pair of horns beneath her hair
Fish-man ancestry on her mother's side
A tail she kept tucked away
explainer: Pudding is part of the Three-Eye Tribe, and the third eye she hid under her hair had made her a target of ridicule.

question: What was Big Mom's true goal behind arranging the wedding between Sanji and Pudding?
correct_answer: To assassinate the entire Vinsmoke family and seize Germa's technology
incorrect_answers: To absorb the Vinsmokes' souls into a single powerful homie
To force Germa 66 into permanent servitude under her
To lure Luffy into a trap and claim his bounty
options: To assassinate the entire Vinsmoke family and seize Germa's technology
To absorb the Vinsmokes' souls into a single powerful homie
To force Germa 66 into permanent servitude under her
To lure Luffy into a trap and claim his bounty
explainer: Big Mom intended to gun down the Vinsmokes at the ceremony and take Germa 66's cloning and science for herself.

question: What are the rank-and-file soldiers of Germa 66, allowing Judge to field a massive expendable army?
explainer: Germa 66's troops are cloned soldiers, which is why Judge treats them as a disposable, endlessly replaceable force.

question: When Big Mom is denied a food she is craving, what dangerous condition does she suffer?
correct_answer: An uncontrollable rampage that endangers even her own family
incorrect_answers: A trance where she relives her childhood at the orphanage
A collapse that leaves her bedridden for days
A total loss of her Devil Fruit powers until she eats
options: An uncontrollable rampage that endangers even her own family
A trance where she relives her childhood at the orphanage
A collapse that leaves her bedridden for days
A total loss of her Devil Fruit powers until she eats
explainer: A denied craving sends Big Mom into a mindless, destructive fit in which she attacks anyone and anything nearby.

question: What triggers the powerful Sulong transformation that Carrot and other Minks can undergo?
incorrect_answers: Entering a berserker rage in battle
Absorbing a large amount of Electro
Being pushed to the brink of death
options: Entering a berserker rage in battle
Absorbing a large amount of Electro
Being pushed to the brink of death
explainer: Minks like Carrot enter their immensely powerful Sulong form by gazing at a full moon.

question: Kaido's Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu lets him transform into what?
explainer: Kaido's Mythical Zoan fruit turns him into a giant azure dragon, the source of his weather-altering powers.

question: During his execution by boiling, how did Kozuki Oden save his nine retainers?
correct_answer: He stood in the boiling cauldron holding them up for a full hour
incorrect_answers: He shielded them with his Haki-hardened body
He fought off the guards long enough for them to flee
He struck a deal with Orochi to spare their lives
options: He stood in the boiling cauldron holding them up for a full hour
He shielded them with his Haki-hardened body
He fought off the guards long enough for them to flee
He struck a deal with Orochi to spare their lives
explainer: Oden endured the boiling oil while holding his retainers aloft for an hour, the feat behind the legend that 'Oden could hold.'

question: Which member of the Nine Red Scabbards turned out to be a spy feeding information to Orochi for years?
explainer: Kanjuro, secretly of the Kurozumi clan, had long been a mole reporting the Scabbards' plans to Orochi.

question: Though the biological child of Kaido, Yamato identifies as whom after being inspired by his journals?
explainer: Moved by Oden's journal, Yamato vowed to become Oden and inherit his will against Kaido.

question: Shogun Kurozumi Orochi's Devil Fruit lets him transform into what creature?
explainer: Orochi's Mythical Zoan, the Hebi Hebi no Mi Model Yamata no Orochi, lets him become an eight-headed serpent, letting him survive several apparent beheadings.

question: Kozuki Oden achieved the rare feat of sailing as a crewmember under which two legendary captains?
correct_answer: Whitebeard and then Gol D. Roger
incorrect_answers: Rocks D. Xebec and then Whitebeard
Gol D. Roger and then Shanks
options: Whitebeard and then Gol D. Roger
Rocks D. Xebec and then Whitebeard
Gol D. Roger and then Shanks
explainer: Oden first sailed with Whitebeard, then joined Roger's crew for the final voyage that reached Laugh Tale.

question: Oden's wife Toki possessed a Devil Fruit with what unique ability?
incorrect_answers: Reversing a person's age to childhood
Erasing memories from a person's mind
Slowing time within a small area
options: Reversing a person's age to childhood
Erasing memories from a person's mind
Slowing time within a small area
explainer: Toki's Toki Toki no Mi let her leap forward in time, which she used to send Momonosuke and the Scabbards twenty years ahead.

question: Momonosuke can transform into a dragon resembling Kaido because he ate what?
correct_answer: An artificial Devil Fruit created by Vegapunk
incorrect_answers: A SMILE fruit stolen from the Beasts Pirates
The very same Mythical Zoan fruit as Kaido
A fruit grown from one of Kaido's shed scales
options: An artificial Devil Fruit created by Vegapunk
A SMILE fruit stolen from the Beasts Pirates
The very same Mythical Zoan fruit as Kaido
A fruit grown from one of Kaido's shed scales
explainer: Momonosuke ate an artificial Devil Fruit engineered by Vegapunk, which is why his dragon form resembles Kaido's.

question: King, Kaido's right-hand man, is one of the last survivors of which nearly extinct race?
explainer: King is a Lunarian, a race once thought extinct, giving him flames and remarkable durability.

question: The three All-Stars, the highest-ranking members serving directly under Kaido, are King, Queen, and who?
explainer: Jack the Drought rounds out the Beasts Pirates' All-Stars alongside King and Queen; the others are lower-ranked Tobiroppo.

question: To help lead the raid on Onigashima, Momonosuke used Shinobu's Devil Fruit to do what?
correct_answer: Age himself twenty years into an adult
incorrect_answers: Shrink down to hide among the enemy
Rot the fortress gates to break in
options: Age himself twenty years into an adult
Shrink down to hide among the enemy
Rot the fortress gates to break in
explainer: Shinobu's Juku Juku no Mi 'ripened' Momonosuke by twenty years, turning the boy into an adult able to fight Kaido.

question: Besides seawater itself, contact with which material drains a Devil Fruit user's strength, which is why the Marines forge it into handcuffs and prison bars?
explainer: Seastone (Kairoseki) emits the same energy as the sea, sapping the power of any Devil Fruit user who touches it, so it is used for restraints and cells.

question: According to established lore, what happens to a person who tries to gain a second power by eating another Devil Fruit?
correct_answer: Their body is torn apart and they die
incorrect_answers: They gain both powers at once
They lose their original power permanently
The second fruit simply has no effect
options: They gain both powers at once
Their body is torn apart and they die
They lose their original power permanently
The second fruit simply has no effect
explainer: Eating a second Devil Fruit causes the user's body to violently rupture and kill them; how Blackbeard wields two powers remains an unexplained mystery.

question: When a Paramecia user like Doflamingo achieves 'awakening,' what new capability does it grant them?
correct_answer: They can apply their power to their surroundings — not just their own body
incorrect_answers: They gain a second Devil Fruit power
They become immune to Armament Haki
They turn their body into an element like a Logia
options: They gain a second Devil Fruit power
They can apply their power to their surroundings — not just their own body
They become immune to Armament Haki
They turn their body into an element like a Logia
explainer: An awakened Paramecia can extend its power onto the environment; Doflamingo turned the ground and buildings of Dressrosa into string.

question: What unusual property sets Zoan-type Devil Fruits apart from the Paramecia and Logia classes?
correct_answer: The fruit itself possesses a will of its own
incorrect_answers: It can be split and shared between two users
It only activates during a full moon
It removes the user's weakness to the sea
options: The fruit itself possesses a will of its own
It can be split and shared between two users
It only activates during a full moon
It removes the user's weakness to the sea
explainer: Zoan fruits are described as having their own will, which lets them animate and grant beast forms to inanimate objects like Funkfreed the elephant sword.

question: A Logia user can turn their body into their element so most blows pass right through them. What is the reliable way to actually strike them?
explainer: Armament Haki (Busoshoku) lets a fighter bypass a Logia's intangibility and land a hit on their real body.

question: Conqueror's Haki (Haoshoku) is famously rare. Roughly how many people are said to be born with the ability to use it?
explainer: Only about one in a million people are said to possess Conqueror's Haki, the will to dominate others, which Luffy first unleashed at the Sabaody Archipelago.

question: Whose Observation Haki was so advanced that he could see a few seconds into the future, an ability Luffy fought hard to copy?
explainer: Katakuri's mastery of Observation Haki let him briefly foresee opponents' movements, and Luffy awakened the same future-sight during their duel on Whole Cake Island.

question: The Poneglyphs are carved in an ancient script almost no one alive can read. Which Straw Hat is one of the rare people able to decipher them?
explainer: Raised among the scholars of Ohara, Nico Robin is one of the only people alive who can read the ancient language of the Poneglyphs.

question: Characters who carry the initial 'D.' in their names, like Luffy, Ace, and Blackbeard, are tied to a mysterious inherited legacy. What is it commonly called?
incorrect_answers: The Oath of the Twenty Kings
options: The Oath of the Twenty Kings
explainer: The 'Will of the D.' is a recurring mystery surrounding those bearing the initial, whom the World Government appears to fear.

question: Who leads the Revolutionary Army in its fight to topple the World Government, and is also revealed to be Luffy's father?
explainer: Monkey D. Dragon, the world's most wanted man, commands the Revolutionary Army and is Luffy's father, as Garp reveals.

question: During the Summit War at Marineford, which of these legendary pirates was one of the reigning Four Emperors (Yonko)?
explainer: Whitebeard was one of the Four Emperors and died at Marineford; the others named were members of the Seven Warlords, not Yonko.

question: After Sengoku stepped down following the Summit War, who took over as the new Fleet Admiral of the Marines?
explainer: Sakazuki won the position of Fleet Admiral after a ten-day duel with Kuzan, who then resigned from the Marines.

question: The Seven Warlords of the Sea (Shichibukai) system was officially abolished by the World Government. At which event was that decision made?
explainer: At the Levely (Reverie), the World Government resolved to dissolve the Warlord system, prompting the Marines to hunt down the former Warlords.

question: In the Final Saga, which former small-time pirate and ex-Warlord was shockingly named one of the new Four Emperors?
explainer: Through a string of misunderstandings and his Cross Guild organization, Buggy was designated one of the Four Emperors with an enormous bounty.

question: The Wano arc showcased a rare advanced use of Conqueror's Haki. What could its masters do that ordinary Conqueror's users could not?
correct_answer: Coat their body and attacks with it to strike enemies harder
incorrect_answers: See several seconds into the future
Harden their skin as hard as steel
options: Coat their body and attacks with it to strike enemies harder
See several seconds into the future
Harden their skin as hard as steel
explainer: Advanced Conqueror's Haki lets a user infuse their strikes with it, dealing internal damage and clashing with other Conqueror's users, as shown by Luffy, Kaido, and Big Mom in Wano.

question: The World Government forbids all research into the roughly 100-year 'Void Century.' What are the only reliable records that can reveal its history?
explainer: The Poneglyphs are indestructible stones inscribed during the Void Century, and studying them is the only way to uncover that forbidden era.

question: During the Void Century, one kingdom opposed the alliance that would become the World Government and was ultimately wiped out. How is this lost kingdom referred to?
explainer: An unnamed powerful kingdom fought the alliance of twenty kingdoms during the Void Century and was destroyed; its ideals may have been left in the Poneglyphs.

question: What was Luffy's very first bounty, issued after he defeated Arlong in the East Blue?
explainer: Luffy's first bounty was 30,000,000 Berries, an unusually high sum for a rookie that stunned the East Blue.

question: After the Straw Hats declared war on the World Government at Enies Lobby, Luffy's bounty leapt to what figure?
explainer: Following the Enies Lobby incident, Luffy's bounty jumped to 300,000,000 Berries.

question: Gol D. Roger, the Pirate King, held the highest bounty ever recorded. As revealed during the Wano arc, what was that figure?
explainer: Roger's bounty of 5,564,800,000 Berries is the highest ever revealed, edging out Whitebeard's 5,046,000,000.

question: When the locations marked on the four Road Poneglyphs are combined, what do they point the way to?
explainer: The four Road Poneglyphs each give a coordinate; crossing them reveals the path to Laugh Tale, the final island where Roger left the One Piece.

question: On which island did the Revolutionary Army keep its headquarters before it was destroyed by Blackbeard's crew?
explainer: Baltigo, the windswept island that served as the Revolutionary Army's base, was attacked and destroyed by the Blackbeard Pirates.

question: Who is the mayor of Foosha Village, constantly exasperated by young Luffy's declaration that he will become a pirate?
explainer: Woop Slap is the mayor of Foosha Village and often scolds Luffy for his pirate ambitions.

question: Who was the kind young woman running Party's Bar in Foosha Village where Shanks and his crew drank during Luffy's childhood?
explainer: Makino is the gentle bartender of Party's Bar in Foosha Village who looked after young Luffy.

question: Which mountain bandit humiliated Shanks in the bar and later kidnapped Luffy, only to be devoured by a Sea King?
explainer: Higuma the Bear was a mountain bandit who taunted Shanks and was eaten by the Lord of the Coast after grabbing Luffy.

question: The timid pink-haired boy Luffy freed from Alvida's ship, who dreamed of becoming a Marine, is named what?
explainer: Koby was Alvida's chore boy until Luffy freed him, after which he set out to join the Marines.

question: The spoiled son of the corrupt Marine Captain Morgan at Shells Town is named what?
explainer: Helmeppo is the arrogant son of Marine Captain Axe-Hand Morgan who lorded over Shells Town.

question: In Syrup Village, Usopp entertained a sickly, wealthy young woman with tall tales. Who was she?
explainer: Kaya was the frail, wealthy heiress of Syrup Village whom Usopp cheered up with wild stories.

question: The Going Merry was designed and gifted through Kaya's devoted household steward. What was his name?
explainer: Merry was Kaya's loyal steward who designed the caravel and after whom the Going Merry was named.

question: Which hypnotist served as first mate of the Black Cat Pirates, able to put people to sleep with a swing of his ring?
explainer: Jango was Captain Kuro's hypnotist first mate who could put others (and himself) to sleep with his chakram.

question: Usopp's habit of shouting that pirates were coming began as a hopeful lie to cheer up his bedridden mother. What was her name?
explainer: Banchina was Usopp's mother, who died of illness while waiting for her husband Yasopp to return.

question: Before losing his leg and opening the Baratie, Zeff was the captain of which pirate crew?
explainer: Zeff was once the captain of the Cook Pirates, known as Red-Leg Zeff, before founding the Baratie.

question: Which member of the Krieg Pirates did Sanji feed despite him being an enemy, earning his loyalty at the Baratie?
explainer: Gin was Don Krieg's combat commander whom Sanji fed when he was starving, earning his gratitude.

question: Which of Don Krieg's officers boasted an 'iron wall' defense and claimed he had never once bled?
explainer: Pearl was a Krieg Pirates officer obsessed with his flawless iron-shield defense until Sanji made him bleed.

question: Which arrogant Marine officer nicknamed 'Iron Fist' first appeared causing trouble at the Baratie?
explainer: 'Iron Fist' Fullbody was a Marine lieutenant who caused a scene at the Baratie restaurant.

question: Who declared himself the strongest man in the East Blue, commanding a large armada until Mihawk shattered his fleet?
explainer: Don Krieg led a fifty-ship pirate armada and considered himself the East Blue's mightiest pirate.

question: Which mustachioed sheriff of Cocoyasi Village helped raise Nami and Nojiko after Bell-mere's death?
explainer: Genzo, the sheriff of Cocoyasi Village, looked after Nami and Nojiko growing up.

question: Nami's blue-haired adoptive older sister, who tends a tangerine grove, is named what?
explainer: Nojiko is Nami's adoptive sister, raised alongside her by Bell-mere in Cocoyasi Village.

question: Arlong, the fishman who enslaved Nami's village, belongs to what species of fishman?
explainer: Arlong is a sawshark fishman, reflected in his saw-shaped nose and jagged teeth.

question: Which six-sword-wielding octopus fishman was a member of the Arlong Pirates?
explainer: Hatchan (Hachi) is the octopus fishman of the Arlong Pirates who fights with six swords.

question: Which ray fishman of the Arlong Pirates was a master of Fishman Karate who fought Sanji?
explainer: Kuroobi is the ray fishman of the Arlong Pirates and a Fishman Karate expert defeated by Sanji.

question: In Loguetown, Zoro was unnerved by a clumsy, sword-loving Marine who looked exactly like his late friend Kuina. Who was she?
explainer: Tashigi is a Marine swordswoman whose resemblance to Kuina repeatedly rattles Zoro.

question: Which of the Seven Warlords of the Sea did Zoro challenge at the Baratie, recognized as the world's greatest swordsman?
explainer: Dracule 'Hawk-Eyes' Mihawk, the world's strongest swordsman, was a Warlord Zoro challenged at the Baratie.

question: Who is the doctor and lighthouse keeper at the Twin Cape entrance to the Grand Line who cares for the whale Laboon?
explainer: Crocus keeps the lighthouse at Twin Cape and tends to Laboon at the Grand Line's entrance.

question: Who is the benevolent king of Alabasta and Princess Vivi's father?
explainer: Nefertari Cobra is Alabasta's king and Vivi's father, framed by Crocodile for the kingdom's drought.

question: After the Drum Island arc, which blue-nosed reindeer joined the Straw Hats as their ship's doctor?
explainer: Tony Tony Chopper, a reindeer trained in medicine on Drum Island, joined the crew as their doctor.

question: Which eccentric elderly doctor, claiming to be well over 140 years old, trained Chopper in medicine on Drum Island?
explainer: Dr. Kureha is the ancient, brilliant doctor of Drum Island who took Chopper in as her apprentice.

question: Which quack doctor took in the outcast Chopper and dreamed of one day curing the entire Drum Kingdom?
explainer: Dr. Hiluluk, a well-meaning quack, adopted Chopper and inspired his dream of becoming a healer.

question: Which gluttonous, tyrannical king fled Drum Island when the Blackbeard Pirates attacked, only to try to reclaim his throne?
explainer: King Wapol abandoned Drum Island during an attack and returned to try to retake it after Wapol's absence.

question: Which Drum Island guard captain opposed King Wapol and later became the island's new leader?
explainer: Dalton, captain of Drum's guard, opposed Wapol's cruelty and became leader of the renamed Sakura Kingdom.

question: Who led the Alabasta rebel army, unaware he was being manipulated by Crocodile, and was Vivi's childhood friend?
explainer: Koza led the rebel army in Alabasta and was Vivi's childhood friend, deceived by Crocodile's scheme.

question: Which of the two guardians of Alabasta's royal family is nicknamed the 'Jackal'?
explainer: Chaka is a royal guardian of Alabasta known as the Jackal, thanks to his Devil Fruit.

question: Who is the loyal captain of Alabasta's royal guard who protected Vivi while posing as the Baroque Works agent Mr. 8?
explainer: Igaram, captain of the royal guard, infiltrated Baroque Works as Mr. 8 to protect Vivi.

question: Which flamboyant okama Baroque Works officer could copy anyone's face and unexpectedly befriended the Straw Hats?
explainer: Mr. 2 Bon Clay, an okama who could copy faces, became an unlikely friend to the Straw Hats.

question: Zoro's fiercest duel in Alabasta was against a stoic agent whose body could turn into steel blades. Which Baroque Works officer?
explainer: Mr. 1 Daz Bones could turn his body into blades, and Zoro learned to cut steel to defeat him.

question: In the port of Nanohana, Luffy reunited with a Whitebeard commander he proudly calls his older brother. Who is he?
explainer: Portgas D. Ace, Luffy's sworn older brother and a Whitebeard commander, appeared in Nanohana.

question: What is the name of Vivi's loyal, oversized riding duck that accompanies the crew through Alabasta?
explainer: Karoo is Vivi's faithful giant Spot-Billed Duck who runs alongside the crew in Alabasta.

question: During the desert trek in Alabasta, the crew rode a stubborn camel that only wanted to carry the women. What was it called?
explainer: Eyelash is the camel the crew found in the Alabasta desert who preferred to carry Nami and Vivi.

question: In Orange Town, a small dog loyally guarded his late owner's pet food shop even against Buggy's crew. What was the dog's name?
explainer: Chouchou is the little dog in Orange Town who fiercely guarded his master's pet food shop.

question: Which former Baroque Works officer asked to join the Straw Hats at the end of Alabasta, becoming their archaeologist?
explainer: Nico Robin, formerly Miss All Sunday, joined the Straw Hats as archaeologist after Alabasta.

question: Which acrobatic swordsman rode a unicycle as an officer of the Buggy Pirates?
explainer: Cabaji the Acrobat is a Buggy Pirates officer who fights with a sword while riding a unicycle.

question: Buggy's beast-tamer officer, who commanded a lion named Richie, is named what?
explainer: Mohji the Beast Tamer is a Buggy Pirates officer who commands his pet lion Richie.

question: Which elderly mayor of Orange Town bravely took up a spear to fight the Buggy Pirates?
explainer: Boodle is the aging mayor of Orange Town who took a stand against Buggy to protect his home.

question: In Whisky Peak, Vivi's Baroque Works partner was a bat-wielding agent obsessed with saying 'Bye bye, baby.' What was his codename?
explainer: Mr. 9 was Miss Wednesday's (Vivi's) partner at Whisky Peak, known for his crowns and metal bats.

question: Brogy was one of the two Elbaf giants locked in a century-long duel on Little Garden. Who was the other?
explainer: Dorry and Brogy are the two Elbaf giants who dueled for a hundred years on Little Garden.

question: Which stubborn old man kept digging for water in the dying oasis town of Yuba, refusing to give up hope?
explainer: Toto, Koza's father, endlessly dug for water to keep the buried oasis town of Yuba alive.

question: Which powerful female Baroque Works agent fought at Whisky Peak as the partner of Mr. 8?
explainer: Miss Monday was a strong Baroque Works agent partnered with Mr. 8 among the Whisky Peak bounty hunters.

question: Mr. 3's young partner in Little Garden used special paints to manipulate emotions. What was her codename?
explainer: Miss Goldenweek, Mr. 3's partner, used her Color Trap paints to influence people's emotions.

question: Arlong exploited Nami's extraordinary talent by forcing her to serve his crew in what role?
explainer: Arlong forced Nami to draw sea charts for him, exploiting her genius for cartography.

question: What position did Sanji hold in the Baratie's kitchen before joining the Straw Hats?
explainer: Sanji worked as the sous chef of the Baratie under Zeff before setting sail with Luffy.

question: Princess Vivi fights with a pair of wire-mounted blades worn on her fingers. What are they called?
explainer: Vivi wields the Peacock Slashers, finger-mounted wire blades she swings with acrobatic skill.

question: Before Alabasta, Usopp built Nami a weather-based staff weapon. What is it called?
explainer: Usopp invented the Clima-Tact for Nami, a segmented staff that manipulates weather phenomena.

question: What codename did Sanji adopt to trick Crocodile over the Transponder Snail while infiltrating Rainbase?
explainer: Sanji posed as 'Mr. Prince' to deceive Crocodile and help the crew escape Rain Dinners.

question: Which Baroque Works officer agent was a giant, slow, nearly silent man partnered with Miss Merry Christmas?
explainer: Mr. 4 was the huge, sluggish agent paired with Miss Merry Christmas in Baroque Works.

question: By what fiery epithet is Ace, the Whitebeard commander who appears in Alabasta, known?
explainer: Portgas D. Ace is known as 'Fire Fist' Ace for his flame powers from the Mera Mera no Mi.

question: Tony Tony Chopper gained human intelligence and speech by eating which Devil Fruit?
explainer: Chopper ate the Hito Hito no Mi, a Zoan fruit that gave the reindeer a human form and intellect.

question: King Wapol of Drum Island could eat almost anything, including metal, thanks to which Devil Fruit?
explainer: Wapol ate the Baku Baku no Mi, letting him devour anything and fuse the materials into his body.

question: Mr. 3 could create and shape hardened wax at will. Which Devil Fruit gave him this power?
explainer: Mr. 3 Galdino ate the Doru Doru no Mi, producing wax as hard as steel to trap his enemies.

question: Which Devil Fruit let Mr. 1 turn any part of his body into a sharpened blade?
explainer: Mr. 1 Daz Bones ate the Supa Supa no Mi, turning his body into blades that could cut steel.

question: Miss Doublefinger could sprout deadly spikes from her body thanks to which Devil Fruit?
explainer: Miss Doublefinger ate the Toge Toge no Mi, letting her grow spikes anywhere on her body.

question: Mr. 2 Bon Clay could take on anyone's exact appearance by touching their face. Which Devil Fruit granted this?
explainer: Bon Clay ate the Mane Mane no Mi, copying a person's face and body by touching them.

question: The Baroque Works agent Mr. 5 could detonate any part of his body, even his breath. Which Devil Fruit did he eat?
explainer: Mr. 5 ate the Bomu Bomu no Mi, letting him make anything from his body, including snot, explode.

question: Miss Valentine could change her body weight from featherlight to crushing. Which Devil Fruit allowed this?
explainer: Miss Valentine ate the Kilo Kilo no Mi, adjusting her weight from 1 to 10,000 kilograms.

question: Miss Merry Christmas could burrow through the ground like a mole. Which Devil Fruit gave her this ability?
explainer: Miss Merry Christmas ate the Mogu Mogu no Mi, a Zoan fruit that turned her into a mole.

question: Pell, the royal guardian who could soar over Alabasta, ate which Zoan Devil Fruit?
correct_answer: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
incorrect_answers: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
options: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
explainer: Pell ate the Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon, letting him transform into a falcon and fly.

question: Chaka, the guardian nicknamed the Jackal, gained his powers from which Zoan Devil Fruit?
correct_answer: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
incorrect_answers: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
options: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
explainer: Chaka ate the Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal, allowing him to transform into a jackal.

question: The Drum Island guard captain Dalton could take on bison-like traits thanks to which Zoan Devil Fruit?
correct_answer: Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
incorrect_answers: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
options: Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Jackal
Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
explainer: Dalton ate the Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison, giving him the strength and horns of a bison.

question: Mr. 4's talking bazooka, Lassoo, was a weapon that had eaten a Zoan Devil Fruit. Which one?
correct_answer: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund
incorrect_answers: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
options: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund
Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
Ushi Ushi no Mi, Model: Bison
Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard
explainer: Lassoo was a bazooka that ate the Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund, becoming a living dog-gun.

question: Crocodile's ability to become and control sand places his Devil Fruit in which of the three main categories?
explainer: Crocodile's Suna Suna no Mi is a Logia, letting him become and manipulate sand itself.

question: Chopper devised a special candy that lets him access extra transformation forms for three minutes at a time. What is it called?
explainer: Chopper invented the Rumble Ball, a candy that temporarily unlocks additional transformation forms.

question: Ace's flame powers, shown when he appears in Alabasta, come from which Logia Devil Fruit?
explainer: Ace ate the Mera Mera no Mi, a Logia fruit that lets him create and become fire.

question: Shanks, the pirate who inspired Luffy, captains which crew?
explainer: Shanks captains the Red Hair Pirates, whose crew stayed in Foosha Village during Luffy's childhood.

question: Captain Kuro secretly led which crew that plotted to attack Syrup Village?
explainer: Kuro was the hidden captain of the Black Cat Pirates who schemed to raid Syrup Village.

question: The fishman crew that occupied Cocoyasi Village and enslaved Nami was called what?
explainer: The Arlong Pirates, a fishman crew, tyrannized the Conomi Islands and forced Nami to serve them.

question: Don Krieg's massive fleet, the largest pirate force in the East Blue, was known as what?
explainer: The Krieg Pirates were Don Krieg's fifty-ship armada, said to be the largest fleet in the East Blue.

question: The secret criminal organization operating on the Grand Line, whose agents use codenames like Mr. 5, was called what?
explainer: Baroque Works was Crocodile's secret syndicate whose agents used numbered Mr. and Miss codenames.

question: Mihawk and Crocodile both belonged to which group of government-sanctioned pirates?
explainer: Both Mihawk and Crocodile were members of the Seven Warlords of the Sea during this era.

question: Who was the very first person to join Luffy's crew, becoming its first mate?
explainer: Roronoa Zoro was the first to join Luffy, freed at Shells Town, and became the crew's swordsman.

question: Which cook joined the Straw Hats after the events at the Baratie?
explainer: Sanji left the Baratie to join the Straw Hats as their cook after Luffy's fight with Don Krieg.

question: What is the official name of Luffy's pirate crew?
explainer: Luffy's crew is officially the Straw Hat Pirates, named for the hat Shanks gave him.

question: The mysterious man Dragon, who saved Luffy from execution in Loguetown, leads which organization?
explainer: Dragon is the leader of the Revolutionary Army, which opposes the World Government.

question: The friendly-seeming town of Whisky Peak was actually populated by bounty hunters secretly serving which syndicate?
explainer: Whisky Peak's welcoming townsfolk were a hundred bounty hunters working for Baroque Works.

question: Within Baroque Works, the boss known only as Mr. 0 was secretly which Warlord?
explainer: Crocodile secretly ran Baroque Works as its boss, Mr. 0, while publicly a hero of Alabasta.

question: Captain Morgan, Smoker, and Tashigi all serve which organization that polices the seas?
explainer: Morgan, Smoker, and Tashigi are all Marines, the naval force of the World Government.

question: In Alabasta, the citizens deceived by Crocodile's drought scheme rose up in a force known as what?
explainer: Believing the king caused the drought, Alabasta's people formed the Rebel Army led by Koza.

question: What did Shanks sacrifice to save young Luffy from the Sea King known as the Lord of the Coast?
explainer: Shanks lost his left arm rescuing Luffy from the Lord of the Coast in Foosha Village.

question: Shanks entrusted his treasured straw hat to Luffy under what promise?
correct_answer: That Luffy would return it once he became a great pirate
incorrect_answers: That Luffy would give it to his own son one day
That Luffy would keep it hidden until the New World
That Luffy would trade it for the One Piece
options: That Luffy would return it once he became a great pirate
That Luffy would give it to his own son one day
That Luffy would keep it hidden until the New World
That Luffy would trade it for the One Piece
explainer: Shanks gave Luffy his straw hat, asking him to return it once he had become a great pirate.

question: What goal does Luffy declare as he sets out to sea from Foosha Village?
correct_answer: To become the King of the Pirates
incorrect_answers: To become the world's greatest swordsman
options: To become the King of the Pirates
To become the world's greatest swordsman
explainer: Luffy's dream is to find the One Piece and become the King of the Pirates.

question: Sanji dreams of finding a legendary sea said to hold fish from all four Blues. What is it called?
explainer: Sanji's dream is to find the All Blue, a mythical sea containing fish from every ocean.

question: What is Nami's personal dream, tied to her talent as a navigator?
correct_answer: To draw a map of the entire world
options: To draw a map of the entire world
explainer: Nami dreams of charting a complete map of the whole world.

question: What was the core of Captain Kuro's scheme in Syrup Village?
correct_answer: Making Kaya sign her fortune over to her butler
incorrect_answers: Marrying Kaya to inherit her estate
options: Making Kaya sign her fortune over to her butler
Marrying Kaya to inherit her estate
explainer: Kuro, posing as the butler Klahadore, plotted to have Kaya will him her fortune and then kill her.

question: When Nami finally broke down and begged Luffy for help against Arlong, what did Luffy do wordlessly?
correct_answer: He placed his straw hat on her head
incorrect_answers: He carried her to the ship
He gave her a new map
options: He placed his straw hat on her head
He carried her to the ship
He gave her a new map
explainer: In answer to Nami's plea, Luffy silently set his straw hat on her head and went to fight Arlong.

question: After tearing at her Arlong Pirates tattoo, what design did Nami replace it with?
explainer: Nami had her Arlong tattoo replaced with a tangerine and a pinwheel, honoring Bell-mere and Genzo.

question: Loguetown is famous as the birthplace and execution site of which legendary figure?
explainer: Loguetown is where the Pirate King Gol D. Roger was born and later publicly executed.

question: How do ships officially enter the Grand Line at Reverse Mountain?
correct_answer: By riding rising currents up and over the mountain
incorrect_answers: By tunneling beneath the Red Line
By crossing the Calm Belt on foot
By being carried over by giants
options: By riding rising currents up and over the mountain
By tunneling beneath the Red Line
By crossing the Calm Belt on foot
By being carried over by giants
explainer: Ships enter the Grand Line by riding water canals up Reverse Mountain and down the other side.

question: The giant whale Laboon repeatedly rams the Red Line at the Grand Line's entrance for what reason?
correct_answer: It is waiting for a crew that promised to come back
incorrect_answers: It is trying to smash into the Grand Line
It is searching for its lost calf
options: It is waiting for a crew that promised to come back
It is trying to smash into the Grand Line
It is searching for its lost calf
explainer: Laboon rams the Red Line waiting for the pirate crew that left it in Crocus's care years ago.

question: Why is an ordinary compass useless on the Grand Line, forcing sailors to rely on a Log Pose?
correct_answer: Each island has its own magnetic field
incorrect_answers: Thick fog always hides the stars
options: Each island has its own magnetic field
Thick fog always hides the stars
explainer: Grand Line islands each emit their own magnetism, so a normal compass fails and a Log Pose is needed.

question: On Little Garden, how did Mr. 3 try to kill the giants and the Straw Hats?
correct_answer: With a giant candle to seal them in wax
incorrect_answers: With a cloud of poison gas
options: With a giant candle to seal them in wax
With a cloud of poison gas
explainer: Mr. 3 used his wax powers and a giant candle set to slowly encase his enemies in stone-hard wax.

question: Why was young Chopper driven away by both the reindeer and the humans of Drum Island?
correct_answer: His blue nose made him an outcast among reindeer
incorrect_answers: He was born unable to walk
He had eaten the village's food stores
options: His blue nose made him an outcast among reindeer
He was born unable to walk
He had eaten the village's food stores
explainer: Chopper's blue nose and human-like changes made him an outcast among both reindeer and people.

question: According to Dr. Hiluluk, when does a person truly die?
correct_answer: When he is forgotten by others
incorrect_answers: When he loses the will to live
options: When he is forgotten by others
When he loses the will to live
explainer: Hiluluk's famous belief was that a person only truly dies when they are forgotten by everyone.

question: As Wapol was defeated at the end of the Drum arc, what miracle appeared over the island?
correct_answer: Pink cherry-blossom snow fell over the island
incorrect_answers: A meteor shower lit the sky
options: Pink cherry-blossom snow fell over the island
A meteor shower lit the sky
explainer: Using Hiluluk's research, Kureha made pink cherry-blossom snow fall over Drum, fulfilling his dream.

question: Crocodile secretly caused Alabasta's drought and framed the king by using what illegal substance?
explainer: Crocodile used banned Dance Powder to pull rain to Nanohana, making it look like the king caused the drought.

question: Why did Princess Vivi infiltrate Baroque Works under the codename Miss Wednesday?
correct_answer: To uncover the identity of the boss threatening her kingdom
options: To uncover the identity of the boss threatening her kingdom
explainer: Vivi joined Baroque Works undercover to learn who its hidden boss was and stop the plot against Alabasta.

question: How did the Straw Hats bid farewell to Vivi without words at the end of Alabasta?
correct_answer: They raised their left arms to show a matching mark
incorrect_answers: They fired the ship's cannons in salute
They each gave her a straw hat
options: They raised their left arms to show a matching mark
They fired the ship's cannons in salute
They each gave her a straw hat
explainer: Rather than speak, the crew raised their left arms bearing the same X mark as a silent goodbye to Vivi.

question: How did Luffy finally overcome Crocodile's sand powers in their last battle?
correct_answer: By wetting his sandy body with liquid
incorrect_answers: By trapping him in the desert heat
By cutting off his golden hook
options: By wetting his sandy body with liquid
By trapping him in the desert heat
By cutting off his golden hook
explainer: Crocodile's sand form cannot hold together when soaked, so Luffy used liquid to land solid blows.

question: Before parting in Alabasta, Ace gave Luffy a slip of paper that could track him. What is it called?
explainer: Ace handed Luffy a Vivre Card, a piece of living paper that points toward its owner's location.

question: After his crushing defeat by Mihawk at the Baratie, what did Zoro vow to Luffy?
correct_answer: To never lose again until he surpasses Mihawk
incorrect_answers: To give up the sword forever
To defeat Mihawk within the year
options: To never lose again until he surpasses Mihawk
To give up the sword forever
To defeat Mihawk within the year
explainer: Beaten by Mihawk, Zoro swore to Luffy he would never lose again until he became the greatest swordsman.

question: Trapped in a cage in Rain Dinners, Luffy and his friends were suspended above a pool of what deadly creatures?
explainer: Crocodile trapped the crew above a pool of Bananawani, giant banana-shaped crocodiles, in Rain Dinners.

question: What is the name of Luffy's home village in the East Blue?
explainer: Luffy grew up in Foosha Village on Dawn Island, where Shanks left him his straw hat.

question: In which town did Luffy first meet Koby and free the captive Roronoa Zoro from a Marine base?
explainer: Shells Town, home to a Marine base run by Captain Morgan, is where Luffy recruited Zoro.

question: In which town did Buggy the Clown seize control, terrorizing its people and the loyal dog Chouchou?
explainer: Buggy's crew occupied Orange Town, where Luffy and Nami fought them alongside Mayor Boodle.

question: In which village did Usopp live and Kaya reside, where the crew received the Going Merry?
explainer: Syrup Village on the Gecko Islands is Usopp's home and where Kaya gifted the crew the Going Merry.

question: What is the name of Nami's home village, which Arlong's crew held under their thumb?
explainer: Cocoyasi Village on the Conomi Islands is Nami's home, oppressed for years by the Arlong Pirates.

question: Cocoyasi Village and Arlong Park are located on which island group?
explainer: Nami's hometown and Arlong Park sit on the Conomi Islands in the East Blue.

question: What is the name of the ocean-going restaurant where Sanji worked as a cook?
explainer: The Baratie is a floating restaurant in the East Blue run by Zeff, where Sanji worked as sous chef.

question: Which town was the Straw Hats' final stop in the East Blue before entering the Grand Line?
explainer: Loguetown was the crew's last East Blue stop, where they resupplied before heading to the Grand Line.

question: The first island the Straw Hats reached on the Grand Line, a town of disguised bounty hunters, was named what?
explainer: Whisky Peak, on Cactus Island, greeted the crew warmly but was full of Baroque Works bounty hunters.

question: Which Grand Line island, despite its gentle name, is a prehistoric jungle of dinosaurs and dueling giants?
explainer: Little Garden is a prehistoric island of dinosaurs where the giants Dorry and Brogy dueled for a century.

question: On which snowy island did the crew find Dr. Kureha and recruit Chopper?
explainer: Drum Island is the winter island ruled by Wapol where the crew met Kureha and Chopper.

question: Vivi's homeland is a vast desert kingdom on the Grand Line. What is it called?
explainer: Alabasta is the desert kingdom ruled by the Nefertari family, Vivi's home.

question: What is the name of Alabasta's royal capital, where the climactic battle against Baroque Works took place?
explainer: Alubarna is Alabasta's capital city, site of the final clash between the crew and Baroque Works.

question: In which Alabasta city was Crocodile's casino base, hiding his headquarters beneath it?
explainer: Rainbase is the Alabasta city housing Crocodile's Rain Dinners casino and secret base.

question: In which Alabasta port town, famous for its perfume, did Luffy reunite with Ace?
explainer: Nanohana is the perfume-scented Alabasta port where Luffy crossed paths with his brother Ace.

question: Which dying Alabasta oasis town, slowly buried by sand, was where Toto kept digging for water?
explainer: Yuba was the oasis town choked by sandstorms where Toto refused to stop digging for water.

question: Vivi showed the crew a once-lush Alabasta city reduced to ruins as proof of the drought. What was it called?
explainer: Erumalu, once the 'City of Green,' was a ruined town showing the toll of Alabasta's drought.

question: Beneath his casino in Rainbase, Crocodile hid his Baroque Works base. What was the casino called?
explainer: Rain Dinners was Crocodile's casino in Rainbase, concealing his headquarters and the Bananawani pool.

question: To reach Dr. Kureha's castle, Luffy climbed which mountain range on Drum Island with his crewmates on his back?
explainer: Luffy scaled the sheer Drum Rockies carrying Nami and Sanji to reach Kureha's castle for treatment.

question: Buggy the Clown carried a surprisingly high bounty for an East Blue pirate. How much was it?
explainer: Buggy's bounty was 15,000,000 Berries, tied to his past with the Roger Pirates.

question: What bounty was placed on Arlong, the fishman who terrorized Nami's home islands?
explainer: Arlong carried a bounty of 20,000,000 Berries, the highest of the East Blue villains Luffy faced.

question: What was the bounty on Don Krieg, admiral of the largest fleet in the East Blue?
explainer: Don Krieg's bounty was 17,000,000 Berries for his rampages across the East Blue.

question: Captain Kuro, who faked his death and posed as a butler, once held what bounty?
explainer: Captain Kuro's former bounty was 16,000,000 Berries before he faked his death to hide.

question: Before it was frozen, what was the Warlord Crocodile's bounty?
explainer: Crocodile's former bounty was 81,000,000 Berries before he became a Warlord of the Sea.

question: After defeating Crocodile in Alabasta, Luffy's bounty was raised to what amount?
explainer: Toppling the Warlord Crocodile raised Luffy's bounty from 30 million to 100,000,000 Berries.

question: Following the Alabasta events, Zoro received his first-ever bounty. How much was it?
explainer: After Alabasta, Zoro was issued his first bounty of 60,000,000 Berries.

question: Mistaken for the crew's pet, Chopper received an insultingly small first bounty of what amount?
explainer: The Marines dismissed Chopper as a mere pet, giving him a token bounty of just 50 Berries.

question: Zoro fights with a distinctive three-sword style, keeping a blade in each hand and one in his mouth. What is this style called?
explainer: Zoro's signature Santoryu, or Three-Sword Style, has him wield a third blade clenched in his teeth.

question: After parting ways with the Roger Pirates, Buggy the Clown eventually formed which crew of his own?
explainer: Buggy, a former Roger Pirates apprentice, went on to captain his own crew, the Buggy Pirates.

question: Before Enel seized power in Skypiea, an aging warrior held the title of 'God' and later fought beside the Straw Hats under the name 'Sky Knight.' Who was he?
explainer: Gan Fall was Skypiea's previous ruler and fought as the Sky Knight riding his bird Pierre.

question: The Sky Knight Gan Fall rode into battle on a loyal steed that had eaten a Devil Fruit. What was the creature's name?
explainer: Gan Fall's steed Pierre ate a Devil Fruit letting it turn into a winged horse.
